seo.title: Building Customer Support with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Azure AI Agent Service + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.
keywords: Azure AI Agent Service customer support

content.intro: Azure AI Agent Service enables building sophisticated customer support agents with RAG, CRM integration, and tool-calling for ticket management and order lookups. Guardrails are essential to ensure response accuracy, PII compliance, policy adherence, and graceful handling of token limits in high-risk enterprise environments. Vex provides middleware validation to mitigate common failures like unvalidated tool results and outdated grounding data.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Azure AI Agent Service
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture features an Azure AI Agent thread that orchestrates customer queries through a RAG pipeline with Bing Search grounding and Azure AI Search for knowledge base retrieval. CRM integration via tools enables ticket creation in Dynamics 365 and order lookups, with Azure OpenAI powering conversational responses. Enterprise security controls filter inputs/outputs while managing thread state across multi-turn interactions.

Agents process user intents by first retrieving context from knowledge bases and CRM, then invoking tools for actions like 'create_ticket' or 'lookup_order'. Responses are grounded against retrieved data to ensure factual accuracy, with escalation logic routing complex issues to human agents. The pipeline handles high-volume support with token-efficient threading and policy enforcement.

Vex integrates as middleware between agent thread execution and response generation, validating tool outputs, grounding data freshness, and compliance before final response delivery.

risks.name: Tool execution results not validated against enterprise policies
risks.description: CRM tools may return unfiltered customer data violating PII policies
risks.mitigation: Vex middleware scans tool outputs for PII and policy violations before response generation

risks.name: Grounding data from Bing Search containing outdated information
risks.description: Search results may reference expired policies or product info causing inaccurate guidance
risks.mitigation: Vex validates grounding data recency and cross-references with knowledge base freshness scores

risks.name: File search returning Azure-specific metadata instead of content
risks.description: Azure AI Search returns file metadata instead of support document content
risks.mitigation: Vex extracts and validates actual content from search results before agent processing

risks.name: Agent thread token limits exceeded without graceful degradation
risks.description: Long support threads exceed context windows causing hallucination or failure
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors token usage and triggers summarization/escalation before limits hit

implementation.title: Initialize Azure AI Agent Project with Vex Middleware
implementation.description: Create agent thread with Vex pipeline integration for customer support workflow
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentProject
from vex_sdk import VexPipeline

project = AgentProject.from_config('azure-ai-customer-support')
vex_pipeline = VexPipeline(
    project_id='cs-001',
    validations=[
        'pii_filtering',
        'policy_compliance',
        'response_accuracy',
        'tone_consistency'
    ]
)

agent_thread = project.create_thread(
    instructions="You are a customer support agent",
    middleware=[vex_pipeline]
)

implementation.title: RAG Tool with Knowledge Base Retrieval
implementation.description: Implement file search tool with Vex content extraction validation
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects.tools import Tool

@Tool(name="kb_search")
def search_knowledge_base(query: str) -> str:
    """Search customer support knowledge base"""
    results = project.search.index('support-kb').search(query)
    # Vex validates content extraction
    validated_content = vex_pipeline.validate_retrieval(results)
    return validated_content

agent_thread.add_tool(kb_search)

vexIntegration.title: PII Filtering Middleware
vexIntegration.description: Real-time PII detection and redaction in agent responses
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

pii_guardrail = VexGuardrail('pii_filtering')

@vex_pipeline.hook('before_response')
def filter_pii(context):
    response = context['agent_response']
    cleaned = pii_guardrail.scan_and_redact(response)
    context['agent_response'] = cleaned
    return context

# Apply to all agent threads
project.apply_middleware(vex_pipeline)
